section: Chapter 13: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Solid Shapes)

questions.question: What are the three dimensions of a solid shape?
questions.explanation: Unlike 2D shapes, 3D solids occupy volume in space.

questions.question: What is a 'Face' of a solid?
questions.correct_answer: The flat surface of a 3D figure
questions.explanation: For example, a cube has six square faces.

questions.question: Define an 'Edge' in a polyhedron.
questions.correct_answer: A line segment where two faces of a solid meet
questions.explanation: Edges form the 'skeleton' or outline of the solid.

questions.question: What is a 'Vertex'?
questions.correct_answer: A corner point where edges meet
questions.explanation: Plural is 'vertices'. A cone has one vertex (apex).

questions.question: What is a 'Net' of a solid?
questions.correct_answer: A 2D pattern that can be folded to form a 3D solid
questions.explanation: It is the 'unfolded' version of a shape.

questions.question: What is the difference between an Oblique and an Isometric sketch?
questions.correct_answer: Oblique sketches show depth visually; Isometric sketches use dots to maintain exact measurements
questions.explanation: Isometric sketches are drawn on a dot grid for accuracy.

questions.question: What is Euler’s Formula for polyhedra?
questions.explanation: Where $F$ is Faces, $V$ is Vertices, and $E$ is Edges.

questions.question: What shape is a horizontal cross-section of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: Slicing a cylinder parallel to its base always results in a circle.

questions.question: What is the 'Plan' view of an object?
questions.explanation: Architects use plan views to show floor layouts.

questions.question: Can a sphere cast a square shadow?
questions.explanation: A sphere always casts a circular shadow regardless of the light's direction.

notes.title: Identifying Polyhedrons
notes.content: Solids with flat faces like cubes and pyramids are polyhedrons; spheres and cylinders are not.

notes.title: Shadow Play
notes.content: A 2D shadow is a projection of a 3D object onto a flat surface.

notes.title: Cross-Sections
notes.content: A vertical slice of a cone through the vertex results in a triangle.

notes.title: Drawing Aids
notes.content: Squared paper is best for oblique sketches, while dot paper is best for isometric ones.

facts.fact: A cube has 11 possible distinct nets.
Engineering drawings rely on front, side, and top views to represent a part accurately.
A cylinder has 2 circular faces and 1 curved surface.
Perspective viewing makes distant parts of a solid look smaller.
